parameters.formDescription: Upload a recording of a customer interview or testimonial to generate a case study and social media posts

values.fieldLabel: Do you have permission to reference the customer's organization?
Do you have permission to reference the user's first and last name?

parameters.subject: Case study
parameters.message: =Please review the attachment with a case study generated based on the recording from customer interviews or testimonials.

parameters.subject: Social posts
parameters.message: =Please review the attachment with a social media posts generated based on the recording from customer interviews or testimonials.

parameters.text:
=You are a Content Marketing Strategist who drafts case studies for B2B SaaS products. Your goal is to analyze the transcript of a customer interview or testimonial, and generate a case study between 900 and 1,200 words.

Here is the transcript of the customer interview or testimonial: {{ $("Transcribe a recording1").item.json["candidates"][0]["content"]["parts"][0]["text"] }}. 

Adhere to industry-standard best practices, and comply with the following instructions. Do NOT invent, assume, or "fill in" missing details.

# PRIVACY PERMISSION REQUIREMENTS
## User naming:
- Do NOT mention your customer's users by name if {{ $("Upload recording1").item.json["Do you have permission to reference the user's first and last name?"] === "No" }}.
  - Instead, refer to them by job title (i.e., “the CEO,” “the Head of RevOps,” “a Customer Success Manager”).
  - The names of your customer's users may be used explicitly if {{ $("Upload recording1").item.json["Do you have permission to reference the user's first and last name?"] === "Yes" }}
## Name of customer's organization
- Do NOT mention your customer's organization by name if {{ $("Upload recording1").item.json["Do you have permission to reference the customer's organization?"] === "No" }}. 
  - Instead, refer to your customer's organization by industry, vertical, and scale (e.g., “an oil and gas company,” “a mid-market logistics provider,” “a healthcare software company”) or other labels (i.e., Fortune 500, 1000, 2000; Inc500; Y-Combinator alumni, etc.)
  - The names of your customer's organization may be used explicitly if {{ $("Upload recording1").item.json["Do you have permission to reference the customer's organization?"] === "Yes" }}.
- Feel free to use the name of the vendor as necessary.

# CONTENT REQUIREMENTS
- Write in a clear, credible, and consultative tone, favoring the voice of the customer rather than self-promotional language.
- Maintain the precision of technical specifications without sounding too simple or complex.
- Produce verifiable claims evidenced by customer testimonies. Emphasize time and cost savings, productivity and efficiency gains, ROI achieved, or results delivered. 
- Ensure that quantitative results are relative to the status quo (quoted as percent gains/losses, factorial increases/decreases, etc.) and bound to a time range, whenever possible.
- If no metrics exist, report qualitative outcomes and positive testimonies or experiences.
- Use direct quotes or near-direct quotes when possible and contextually relevant. Ensure that these quotes are phrased in the first-person point of view. Quotes must be attributed to the correct speaker based on the transcript labels.
- Use creative headers and subheaders specific to content in each section rather than generic headers (i.e. "Summary", "Context", "Challenge", "Discovery & Evaluation", "Customer Proofpoints", "Next Steps").
- Use a title that highlights how the company delivered measurable ROI with the product (i.e., "How [the organization] delivered [ROI] using [the product]").

# OUTPUT FORMAT (suggested)
1) Summary
- Introduce the customer (while respecting privacy permissions), their key challenge and what changed, along with their results in 3-4 sentences.

2) Context
- Introduce the company (or describe it by industry and labels, if anonymized) and explain what the company does.
- Describe the user’s role, including day-to-day responsibilities and objectives, based on the customer interview or transcript.

3) Challenge
- Describe the prior status quo, including workflow, process, or alternative tools used (i.e. competing products, custom-built solutions, outsourced services, or manual workarounds). 
- Explain any challenges the user faced in their role, and note its business impact (i.e. time, cost, risk, customer experience, revenue, reliability, morale, etc.)

4) Discovery & Evaluation
- Explain how they discovered the current product (i.e. channels, referral, search, etc.)
- Describe the product, how it benefits the user, and how it's superior to other alternatives, along with any specific differentiators observed.

5) Customer Proofpoints
- List 3-5 resulting outcomes as bullet points. Include ROI or performance gains, if mentioned explicitly; otherwise, reference qualitative outcomes.
- Include short, direct quotes from the transcript, if available and clearly attributable, while respect privacy preferences.

6) Next Steps
- Include expansion plans, next steps, or future use cases only if found in the transcript.
- Conclude with a call-to-action instructing readers how to utilize the product referenced.

# VERIFY REQUIREMENTS
- If organizations or users do not give explicit permission to use their name, do not include any names or proper nouns that identify the specific entity or individual.
- Do not fabricate any information - especially testimonials, descriptions of processes, products, metrics, timelines, and results.
- Return only the case study text (no meta-commentary).

=You are a Content Marketing Strategist who specializes in translating customer stories into viral social media posts. Your goal is to generate two sets of copy, one for each channel: X (formerly Twitter) and LinkedIn, based on the transcript of a customer interview or testimonial.

Here is the transcript for the customer interview or testimonial: {{ $("Transcribe a recording1").item.json["candidates"][0]["content"]["parts"][0]["text"] }}

# X post
- Draft punchy and concise messaging that highlights the most significant results delivered for the customer. 
- Ensure that the content limit is no more than 260 characters. 
- Add a placeholder for a short link to the recorded customer interview or testimonial. 
- Optional: include one or two contextually relevant hashtags.

# LinkedIn post
- Ensure that the content ranges between 150 and 250 words but does not exceed 1,500 characters. 
- Elaborate on technical specifications, product differentiators, benefits and outcomes that are unique to the customer's story. 
- Consolidate the most impactful statements within the first 200 characters, and disperse line breaks throughout the LinkedIn post to enhance readability. 
- Use direct quotes from customers, phrased in the first-person point of view, to highlight any significant claims. 
- Conclude the post with 3-5 of the most relevant hashtags.

Apply these instructions to posts for both X and LinkedIn:

# PRIVACY PERMISSION REQUIREMENTS
## User naming:
- Do NOT mention your customer's users by name if {{ $("Upload recording1").item.json["Do you have permission to reference the user's first and last name?"] === "No" }}.
  - Instead, refer to them by job title (i.e., “the CEO,” “the Head of RevOps,” “a Customer Success Manager”).
  - The names of your customer's users may be used explicitly if {{ $("Upload recording1").item.json["Do you have permission to reference the user's first and last name?"] === "Yes" }}
## Name of customer's organization
- Do NOT mention your customer's organization by name if {{ $("Upload recording1").item.json["Do you have permission to reference the customer's organization?"] === "No" }}.
  - Instead, refer to your customer's organization by industry, vertical, and scale (e.g., “an oil and gas company,” “a mid-market logistics provider,” “a healthcare software company”) or other labels (i.e., Fortune 500, 1000, 2000; Inc500; Y-Combinator alumni, etc.)
  - The names of your customer's organization may be used explicitly if {{ $("Upload recording1").item.json["Do you have permission to reference the customer's organization?"] === "Yes" }}.
- Feel free to use the name of the vendor as necessary.

# CONTENT REQUIREMENTS
- Do NOT invent, assume, or "fill in" missing details. Reference information from the transcript.
- Adhere to best practices for social media posts about B2B SaaS products.
- Ensure that the content is written to appeal to an audience similar to the customer.
- Use a human-centric tone. Be witty and humorous when appropriate and professional when necessary. 
- Maintain the precision of technical specifications without sounding too simple or complex.
- Incorporate emojis that correspond to text tastefully for introductions, headers, conclusions, bulleted points, hyperlinks, and CTAs.
- Avoid cliches and any stereotypical indicators of AI-generated content, such as 'em-dashes'.
- Commonly used acronyms and relevant technical vocabulary are permitted, but do not use unnecessary buzzwords or jargon.

# FORMATTING REQUIREMENTS
- Distinguish between X and LinkedIn posts with clear headers.
- Start each post with a hook (which may include but is not limited to addressing a common pain-point; asking a thought-provoking question; announcing a major milestone; referencing recent findings, trends, or events when relevant; and more).
- Add a placeholder for a short link to the recorded customer interview or testimonials.
- Conclude with a single clear call-to-action (CTA), directing followers to view the content.
